title: Combat oracle guard AI-state divergence native-vs-stock on Dam (pos/actiontype/room/health/target_visible); roster matches 36/36

history.note: Both-sides Dam capture (FID-0032 verify), 138 motion-anchored frames. Guard ROSTER matches perfectly: 36 guards common on both sides every frame. Divergences are a stable subset (NOT alignment drift - counts flat across frames 0..130): guard pos ~10/36 differ consistently (guards.pos 0/1/2 = 1379/1295/1378 field-hits), actiontype ~1-3/36 (293), room (217), health (115), target_visible (104). This is the core combat/AI parity signal seeding FID-0011 (H4 actiontype dispatch/H5 background-AI) and FID-0013 (F2). ACT follow-up needs a frame-LOCKED capture (identical input timeline both sides, not motion-anchored) to isolate each guard's genuine divergence and classify port-defect vs n64-quirk per guard. dam_forward_stop is a no-combat route so shots/hits/projectiles stayed 0/0 (FID-0047/0048 unexercised here).

Combat route dam_combat_guard6 (shots 7, projectiles 2). Both-sides Dam capture, 218 aligned frames (--align move). FIRST combat-triggered divergence: guard chrnum=6 identical both sides frames 0..35 then aligned frame 36 (~16f after first shot at f20): target_visible stock=0->native=1 co-incident with actiontype stock=1->native=8. Native guard 6 acquires Bond as visible target + dispatches actiontype 8; N64 stock guard stays unaware (tvis 0/act 1). First field = target_visible (read of ChrRecord.lastseetarget60). Hypothesis: native see-target/alertness update cadence + actiontype-8 dispatch in chr.c guard tick (feeding lastseetarget60) + stan.c LOS gate over-/earlier-perceives vs ASM -> port-defect candidate, not n64 quirk. Absolute-first divergence is frame 0 on BACKGROUND guards (chr 2,3 actiontype/room/health/pos) = pre-existing stable ~10/36 subset, combat-independent. Fix deferred to ACT iteration.
